text: DCO-Internal Defensive Measures (DCO-IDM) are authorized within the commander's own networks. Offensive Cyberspace Operations (OCO) require SecDef authorization. Detection of adversary pre-positioning should trigger immediate DCO-IDM and escalation to CISA for critical infrastructure sectors.

Posture tiers align to adversary kill-chain stage: reconnaissance → deny access; initial access → isolate and evict; persistence → hunt and restore; pre-impact staging → emergency protective measures and notification to sector partners.

Persistent engagement — maintaining an active posture rather than waiting for adversary action — is preferred over reactive response. PRC-affiliated actors demonstrating pre-positioning behavior in critical infrastructure networks represent a strategic deterrence capability requiring immediate DCO-IDM response.

Phase 0 (Shape) and Phase I (Deter) operations include persistent cyberspace engagement. Adversary infrastructure pre-positioning during Phase 0 is a strategic indicator of Phase II (Seize Initiative) preparation. Commanders must assess whether cyber activity signals imminent kinetic escalation.

Escalation management requires understanding adversary red lines and decision calculus. Cyber operations that target critical national infrastructure may trigger responses across all domains. Interagency coordination with CISA, NSA, and FBI is mandatory for critical infrastructure incidents under E.O. 13800.
